summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the 'Building Character' series with a message on living a life of sacrifice. He opened by reflecting on the sacrificial examples of his mother and wife, contrasting them with the extreme sacrifices of modern missionaries to illustrate the call to a higher standard. The core of the sermon was an exposition of Romans 12:1, which urges believers to offer their bodies as a living sacrifice. Pastor Travis explained that true worship is not merely singing but a daily, costly surrender of one's will, desires, and comforts to God. He broke the verse into four parts: the motivation of God's mercy, the offering of our whole selves, the call to be holy (set apart) and pleasing to God, and the understanding that this surrender is our logical and reasonable act of worship. The message challenged listeners to examine what areas of life they were withholding from God and to lay them on the altar, emphasizing that Christlike character is impossible without sacrifice. The sermon concluded with an invitation for salvation and a call for believers to fully surrender specific strongholds to God.
mainPoints: Christlike character requires sacrifice, which means laying down our own desires, dreams, and comforts for God and others.
Our logical and proper worship, motivated by God's mercy, is to offer our entire selves—body, mind, and spirit—as a living sacrifice to God.
Being holy means being set apart and different from the world's patterns, not conforming but being transformed to reflect Christ's light.

versesMentioned.text: Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.

Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, whom you have received from God? You are not your own; you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies.
But you are a chosen people, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, God’s special possession, that you may declare the praises of him who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light.
Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will.
But Daniel resolved not to defile himself with the royal food and wine, and he asked the chief official for permission not to defile himself this way.
Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.

Monday.scripture: Romans 12:1 (NIV) - 'Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice...'
Monday.action: Begin your day by writing down one specific way God has shown you mercy. Let that gratitude be your motivation for the day.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for your new mercies this morning. Help me to live today in view of your great love and forgiveness.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 6:20 (NIV) - '...you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies.'
Tuesday.action: Choose one physical aspect to honor God with today (e.g., what you watch, what you eat, how you rest). Make one intentional, honoring choice.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I acknowledge that my body is your temple. Help me to honor you with my physical choices today in a way that pleases you.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Peter 2:9 (NIV) - 'But you are a chosen people, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, God’s special possession...'
Wednesday.action: In a conversation or situation today, consciously choose a response that is 'set apart'—characterized by grace, patience, or love where the world might respond differently.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, remind me today that I am set apart for you. Give me the courage to be different in a way that reflects your light.

Thursday.scripture: Romans 12:2 (NIV) - 'Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind.'
Thursday.action: Identify one 'pattern of this world' you tend to conform to (e.g., anxiety, criticism). Replace 15 minutes of media consumption with scripture reading or prayer.
Thursday.prayer: Father, transform me by renewing my mind. Help me to reject worldly patterns and cling to your truth today.

Friday.scripture: Daniel 1:8 (NIV) - 'But Daniel resolved not to defile himself...'
Friday.action: Make a 'Daniel resolution.' Identify one thing you need to lay on the altar for God. Write it down and pray over it, symbolically surrendering it.
Friday.prayer: Lord, like Daniel, I resolve to live for you. I lay [name the thing] on your altar today. Give me the strength to leave it there.

Saturday.scripture: John 15:13 (NIV) - 'Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.'
Saturday.action: Perform one act of sacrificial love for someone in your family or community. Let it be something that costs you time, comfort, or resources.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, you laid down your life for me. Show me how to lay down my life in love for someone else today. Let my action be an offering to you.
